purpose: This policy applies when <<LAB_NAME>> performs frozen-section processing in support of Mohs micrographic surgery. Required by 42 CFR 493.1232 (specimen integrity) and 42 CFR 493.1291 (test report).

cfr_text_blocks.label: Standard: Specimen identification and integrity
cfr_text_blocks.verbatim: The laboratory must establish and follow written policies and procedures to ensure positive identification and optimum integrity of a patient's specimen from the time of collection or receipt through completion of testing and reporting of results.

scope: <<LAB_NAME>> histology personnel supporting Mohs surgery and the Mohs surgeon.
policy_statements: Mohs specimens arrive labeled with the patient identifier, anatomic location, stage (Mohs map number), and orientation marks placed by the Mohs surgeon.
Histology processes the specimen: embedding to maintain orientation, frozen sectioning, staining, and slide return to the Mohs surgeon for interpretation.
Turnaround time from receipt to slide-ready is tracked as a quality indicator; the lab's target is set by agreement with the Mohs surgeon.
Slides and tissue are retained per the agreed retention with the Mohs practice (typically slides 10 years per 42 CFR 493.1105(a)(7); paraffin blocks 2 years; wet tissue per practice protocol).
Any specimen handling error (orientation lost, specimen damaged, mismatched ID) is reported to the Mohs surgeon immediately.
procedure_steps: Receive specimen with surgeon-provided map and orientation marks; verify identity.
Embed maintaining orientation; section per the lab's Mohs procedure (en face en bloc embedding is common).
Stain and return slide to surgeon for interpretation.
Track and document turnaround; address any handling errors immediately.
definitions: Skin cancer surgery technique using staged excision with en face frozen section margin assessment between stages.
Sutures or ink applied by the surgeon to mark anatomic orientation of the excised tissue; preserved by histology embedding.
